description: The Telegram auto-launch task is reinitialized as a standalone harness separate from the completed repo_sync recovery work.
steps: Open PLAN.json and verify it only describes Telegram auto-launch work for coding tasks.
Open PROGRESS.md and verify the reboot entry records the current baseline and the decision to keep CLI run semantics unchanged.
Confirm the new task scope is limited to Telegram start-command launch behavior and its focused regressions.

description: Telegram private-chat `开始编程` automatically launches the newly created coding task when a launcher is available.
steps: Send a Telegram private-chat start command in a focused routing test with a fake launcher attached.
Verify nanobot creates the task and immediately calls the launcher for that new task id.
Confirm the Telegram acknowledgement reports the launched status instead of leaving the task queued.

description: Automatic Telegram launch reuses the existing coding-task launcher path rather than duplicating worker startup logic in the router.
steps: Inspect the Telegram start-command handler after the change.
Verify it delegates launch to the shared launcher abstraction already used by `nanobot coding-task run`.
Confirm no second worker-start implementation or ad hoc tmux logic is added to the router.

description: If no launcher is wired into the runtime, Telegram start commands still fall back to create-only behavior instead of failing.
steps: Send a Telegram private-chat start command through a runtime that only has a manager.
Verify the task is still persisted successfully.
Confirm the acknowledgement remains usable and does not claim a worker was launched.

description: If automatic launch raises an exception, nanobot preserves the created task and returns a clear Telegram failure message instead of silently dropping work.
steps: Send a Telegram private-chat start command with a launcher stub that raises.
Verify the task record still exists in the coding-task store.
Confirm the Telegram reply explains that creation succeeded but launch failed.

description: Focused regression tests cover Telegram auto-launch success, create-only fallback, and launch failure behavior.
steps: Run the focused coding-task routing test files.
Verify the new assertions cover both the happy path and the failure boundary.
Confirm the regression suite remains scoped away from unrelated global pytest failures.

description: User-facing documentation explains that Telegram `开始编程` now launches Codex immediately while CLI create/run remains available as an explicit path.
steps: Open the updated README coding-task section.
Verify it states that Telegram start commands now create and launch automatically.
Confirm the docs still mention the CLI create/run workflow as an explicit alternative.
